rulings.Case Title: DISCOVER BANK VS. LISA P. CHOW et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Claim Of Exemption
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, October 31, 2011, Line 1. Hearing On Claim Of Exemption (of claimant Lisa Chow). Denied in part. The judgment debtor failed to show that her entire monthly income is needed for her support. The levying officer is directed to release $350 to the judgment creditor for payment on the judgment. The remaining amount of $690.23 shall be released to the judgment debtor. Beginning November 1, 2011, judgment debtor shall make monthly payments of $350 on the judgment until it is satisfied in full. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: KARIN GREIER VS. PHILIP SODERQUIST et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Response And Responses
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, October 31, 2011, Line 3. DEFENDANT PHILIP SODERQUIST'S Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Response And Responses. Denied as to interrogatories 9-12 and 14-15 and granted as to interrogatory 13. Interrogatories 9-12 and 15 are not proper interrogatories. Interrogatory 14 is duplicative of interrogatory 13. Interrogatory 13 is proper and should be answered in full. Sanctions are denied. Neither side has exhibited exemplary behavior with respect to the discovery requests at issue. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: SHAMA TAHIR et al VS. STATE OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Order Compelling On Failure To Permit Inspection Of Docs In Accordance W/ Responses To Req Production
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, October 31, 2011, Line 5. PLAINTIFFS SHAMA TAHIR, TANVEER TAHIR AND WASIM TAHIR'S Motion For Order Compelling On Failure To Permit Inspection Of Documents In Accordance With Responses To Request for Production. Denied. Although the motion does not raise a non-justiciable political question, plaintiffs have not articulated any reason why the confidentiality agreement proposed by the State of California impedes plaintiffs from pursuing their claims in this action. rulings.Case Title: DALE C. WEBB VS. LORRAINE O. LEGG, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS GENERAL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Morgan Stanley Smith Barney, Llc'S Petition To 1) Compel Arbitration And 2) Stay Action
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, October 31, 2011, line 10, Defendant Morgan Stanley Smith Barney, Llc'S Petition To 1) Compel Arbitration And 2) Stay Action IS Denied. Assuming without deciding that the arbitration clause in the Client Agreement is applicable to plaintiff¿s claims against Morgan Stanley Smith Barney (MSSB), that clause does not cover claims between plaintiff and M. Legg. Plaintiff¿s claims against MSSB and Ms. Legg are interrelated (see third through seventh claims in the complaint) and there is a real possibility of conflicting rulings if plaintiff¿s claims against Ms. Legg and Morgan Stanley are decided in two different fora. (CCP 1281.2(c)). In particular, if the motion was granted, then plaintiff would face the risk that in different fora Ms. Legg and MSSB would defend based on the alleged misconduct of the other. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/HEK

rulings.Case Title: SUSAN Y F LEE VS. WAH-BUN LEE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, October 31, 2011, line 6, DEFENDANTS TONY LEE AND CHAN LAI-SAU'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS Sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the seventh cause of action for conspiracy, sustained without leave to amend as to the eighth causes of action for wrongful eviction, and overruled as to the ninth cause of action for negligence. Civil conspiracy is not a claim, but rather a basis to impute liability to someone who did not personally commit the alleged wrongful conduct. The wrongful eviction claim is time-barred. The negligence claim is adequately pled. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: SUSAN Y F LEE VS. WAH-BUN LEE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion And Motion To Stay Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, October 31, 2011, line 6, DEFENDANT WAH-BUN LEE'S Ntc Of Motion And Motion To Stay Proceedings IS Granted in part. The deposition of Wah-Bun Lee may not occur until the later of January 1, 2013 or the conclusion of any criminal proceedings against Mr. Lee based on the same allegations as set forth in the complaint filed by the plaintiff. No other portion of the case is stayed. However, Mr. Lee is not precluded from asserting in good faith the Fifth Amendment as to any written discovery served on him. It is expected that, if no criminal case is brought against Mr. Lee by December 31, 2012, this case should be in a position to be tried in early 2013 shortly after Mr. Lee¿s deposition will have taken place. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/HEK

rulings.Case Title: WEST GROUP REAL ESTATE, INC., A CALIFORNIA et al VS. KARIN THURM et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, October 31, 2011, line 9, DEFENDANT KARIN THURM MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT IS Granted. Defendant satisfied her burden that the complaint is based on protected conduct, which is her filing of the prior lawsuit. Plaintiffs did not meet their burden of showing any merit to their claims. The complaint is, in legal effect, a malicious prosecution suit, but lacks the required elements of such a claim. Defendant awarded $2910 (reasonable fees of $2500 plus costs of $410). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
